items.title: Kingpin's private phone calls break open Mongols' 'culture of silence'

children.content: Detectives say bikies are recruiting inside Northern Territory prisons, as a kingpin's tapped private phone calls offer a rare glimpse into the underground world of outlaw motorcycle gangs.

gallery.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

thumbnail.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

items.title: Boy, 16, killed, two others injured in Wadeye quad bike crash

children.content: A 16-year-old boy has been killed in a quad bike crash that also injured a 12-year-old boy and a 19-year-old man in Wadeye on Wednesday.

image.altText: Close up image of an NT Police sign, behind barbed wire fence, with left of frame slightly obstructed by palm tree leaves.

items.title: Police 'not fully prepared' for violent scene they were called to, NT inquest hears

children.content: An inquest hears evidence from a senior police officer who ran into a room to disarm a mother who was self-harming inside a Darwin crisis accommodation facility, describing the incident as "one of the worst days" of her policing career.

image.altText: A police car outside the Darwin Local Court.

items.title: 101yo Bombing of Darwin veteran wants Australians to know what unfolded

children.content: At 101 years old, Bombing of Darwin survivor Terrance Byrne wants Australians to know exactly what unfolded during the World War II attack on Darwin — still the single largest foreign attack on Australian soil.

gallery.altText: An older man sitting in front of table of framed black and white pictures.
A black and white photo shows a huge crater in the ground, alongside three undamaged oil tanks located near the coastline.
An army crew operating a large searchlight, the uniformed men are looking towards and pointing up at the night sky.

thumbnail.altText: An older man sitting in front of table of framed black and white pictures.
A black and white photo shows a huge crater in the ground, alongside three undamaged oil tanks located near the coastline.
An army crew operating a large searchlight, the uniformed men are looking towards and pointing up at the night sky.

items.title: 'How many beers have you had?' Flood evacuees claim racial profiling

children.content: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

image.altText: Signs attached to black fencing reads: no alcohol, no visitors

items.title: New $30.5m funding package to help repair NT roads damaged by flooding

children.content: The Northern Territory government has announced $30.5 million in funding to repair roads damaged by wet season flooding across the territory.

image.altText: A remote road collapsed to one side.

items.title: Central Australian community sues government over climate-unsafe housing

children.content: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say scorching outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they're suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

gallery.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.
An Indigenous woman and two children sitting on a mattress that rests on the flood inside a home.
A box air conditioner on the outside of a house, balanced on an ironing board and a table.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.
An Indigenous woman and two children sitting on a mattress that rests on the flood inside a home.
A box air conditioner on the outside of a house, balanced on an ironing board and a table.

items.title: Housing plan aims to attract more students to international 'gateway' city

children.content: A towering 14-storey addition to Darwin's skyline is expected to ease housing stress for university students, with $70 million invested to tackle the city's competitive rental market.

image.altText: A sketch of a multi-storey building based in the city, the CDU logo can be seen on the side of the accommodation tower.

items.title: Why this man risked crocodiles, storms and jail to fish in Australian waters

children.content: Why do Indonesian crews keep fishing illegally in Australian waters when the activity is so perilous?

image.altText: A man in a green shirt looks to the right.

images.altText: Three ships and a small dinghy on a body of water.
Large container ship on waterways in the Strait of Hormuz

image.altText: Masked men in sand-coloured army fatigues climbing a ladder off the side of a ship.

children.content: Iran has said it will not reopen the Strait of Hormuz as long as the US naval blockade remains in place. Follow live.

items.title: Iran attacks three cargo ships in strait, US Navy Secretary departs role

updates.title: PM 'absolutely happy' with indefinite ceasefire extension
PM says government will know four to six weeks ahead of possible fuel restrictions

items.title: Actor suing Rebel Wilson agrees bath incident was 'strange and bizarre'

children.content: A young actor suing Rebel Wilson for defamation has given details in court about an incident at Bondi Beach, which she claims the Hollywood star has since mischaracterised.

image.altText: Charlotte MacInnes arriving outside court

items.title: 'Bordering on bullying': Voss hits out at coverage of Hollands

children.content: Michael Voss has called out the media for the way it has covered Elijah Hollands's medical episode, saying the coverage of the 23-year-old "bordered on bullying".

image.altText: Michael Voss and Elijah Hollands stand next to each other

items.title: 'Not a nice place to live at the moment': Kununurra residents at wits' end

children.content: Stolen cars and accommodation break-ins have led to growing frustration in Western Australia's north.

image.altText: A couple stand looking sternly with a car behind them

items.title: Every Glock handgun issued to Qld police to be tested

children.content: The Glock pistol has been identified to have a potential fault that could cause them to fire multiple shots instead of one.

image.altText: Close up of Queensland Police Service-issued black gun in holster on navy pants

items.title: Authors leave publisher after kids book cancelled for illustrator's comments

children.content: Writers including Evelyn Araluen and Randa Abdel-Fattah have left University of Queensland Press after the publisher axed publication of an Indigenous kids book over its illustrator's past 'anti-Semitic' comments.

gallery.altText: A young woman with cropped hair, wearing large gold earrings and a scarf over her shoulders.

thumbnail.altText: A young woman with cropped hair, wearing large gold earrings and a scarf over her shoulders.

avatar.alt: Hannah Story, a blonde woman sitting outside smiles. A dog is at her feet, and she wears a T-shirt that reads "MIRANDA JULY".

items.title: Family's eviction exposes a complex tussle between renters and landlords

children.content: Evicting a tenant without cause offers landlords a simple way out, but the issue can be much more complex than it appears. One such example of that complexity is Eleanor Kahlo's case of her no-grounds eviction.

image.altText: A man with a vest on and a clipboard watches as Eleanor walks through a hallway.

items.title: Farmers say an easy solution to the mouse plague has been rejected

children.content: Farmers are pushing for stronger bait approval as mice numbers soar out of control in Western Australia, but stronger poisons
are being rejected by the national chemical regulator.

image.altText: Feral mice in a bucket.

items.title: Leading Trump envoy suggests FIFA replace Iran with Italy at World Cup

children.content: As the US president feuds with Pope Leo, one of his top envoys suggests giving Italy a World Cup spot could mend fences.

image.altText: Italy's men's football team poses for a photo

items.title: Small WA town takes centre stage for Netflix series filming

children.content: There is a buzz about town in a small community north of Perth as filming begins for a new Netflix series.

image.altText: A film crew in front of beige buildings.

items.title: Seeds can 'hear' the sound of raindrops, new study suggests

children.content: Scientists say they have the first direct evidence that seeds can sense the sound of rain, and will sprout faster when they do.

gallery.altText: Green seedling shoots covered in tiny water droplets in dark soil.

thumbnail.altText: Green seedling shoots covered in tiny water droplets in dark soil.

items.title: Genesis Owusu goes out of his way to connect with fans — and detractors

children.content: Genesis Owusu today releases his new track Life Keeps Going from his upcoming third album Redstar Wu & the Worldwide Scourge, as he encourages engagement with people who don't agree with his political views.

image.altText: Genesis Owusu bare-chested on stage singing into a microphone, yellow background, Kente cloth from waist down

items.title: A dozen new offences added to LNP's adult crime, adult time laws in Qld

children.content: Conspiring to commit murder, unlawful stalking and riot are among 12 new offences that juveniles can now be sentenced as adults for.

image.altText: Large billboard of man with politcal slogans

items.title: Removing plastic from our food chain yields quick results, researchers say

children.content: Perth researchers find levels of plastic chemicals in the body can be reduced within a week by avoiding plastic food packaging and kitchen utensils.

items.title: New AI threat looms but Australian firms don't have access needed to prepare

children.content: Local banks, power providers and infrastructure firms do not have access to test their systems against a powerful new AI cybersecurity risk, Anthropic's Mythos, which has not been made public.

image.altText: Anthropic logo on a smartphone screen

items.title: Pacific leaders 'totally blindsided' by declaration of fuel emergency

children.content: Confusion and some frustration remains after a key Pacific leader announced an emergency declaration had been agreed to over fuel, despite other leaders not knowing about it.

image.altText: A man in a suit rests his hand on his chest, standing in front of Solomon Island and China flags.

items.title: This man plays the last post to a different fallen Anzac every day

children.content: Adam Farquharson feels the honour of Anzac Day every day as he plays to a different veteran's grave.

gallery.altText: A man in black plays a silver instrument in a graveyard

thumbnail.altText: A man in black plays a silver instrument in a graveyard

items.title: Two women on the hunt to find every soldier settler name in El Arish

children.content: Anzac Day holds meaning for every Australian, but for Marie Carman and Lorelei King, it is another reminder of their deeply personal mission to uncover the forgotten stories of the soldiers who helped build their town.

image.altText: Two older women smile together out the front of the El Arish Diggers Museum.

items.title: 'Going out for coffee not a luxury': NDIS families grapple with planned cuts

children.content: An average cut of $7,000 to NDIS participant plans could hurt social supports, providing the "only interaction with the outside world" for some people with disability, advocates warn, as the minister concedes the savings will have a material impact on participant plans.

image.altText: A man in a wheelchair playing basketball

items.title: Police officer given 'golden pass' on rape allegations, victim says

children.content: When Tasmania Police misidentified Laura as a family violence perpetrator, she had no idea she'd have to fight for them to hold her abuser — a serving officer — to account. Now, she's fighting for change.

gallery.altText: A police officer's torso showing a body cam

thumbnail.altText: A police officer's torso showing a body cam

items.title: How a junior basketballer will become Wallaroos' most-capped player

children.content: Rugby was not on Michaela Leonard's radar when she was a child in Canberra, but she is about to achieve a historic milestone when the Wallaroos meet the Black Ferns on Anzac Day.

image.altText: Michaela Leonard during a break in a Wallaroos Test in 2025.

items.title: 'Not a great look': Piastri weighs in on Verstappen's F1 frustrations

children.content: Australian star Oscar Piastri speaks as Formula 1 champion Max Verstappen looks increasingly likely to walk away from the sport.

image.altText: An image of three F1 drivers speaking during an interview in pit lane.

items.title: Aussies crash in Madrid as Kasatkina sinks to new ranking low

children.content: Daria Kasatkina's difficult WTA Tour season has continued with an agonising defeat at the Madrid Open, as all four Aussie women crash out in the opening round.

image.altText: Daria Kasatkina gets ready to play a shot

items.title: Thousands sign petition against McDonald's Phillip Island restaurant plan

children.content: The fast food giant is facing backlash after lodging a planning permit application to build a new restaurant in tourist hotspot Cowes.

image.altText: Artist render of a McDonald's restaurant.

items.title: No more green bin pick-ups for some residents amid fuel uncertainty

children.content: The Upper Lachlan Shire Council says it cannot afford to be caught off-guard if a diesel shipment does not arrive, and must reserve fuel for essential services like road grading or red bin collections.

gallery.altText: Council bins with green lids on suburban street.

thumbnail.altText: Council bins with green lids on suburban street.

items.title: Alleged drink driver detected at 151km/h with child in ute, police say

children.content: A man who was allegedly drink driving with a child inside a speeding ute in Adelaide's south last night will face court after being stripped of his licence, police say.

image.altText: An SA Police logo.
